content.text:
## Design Section 1: Database Schema

New `release_todos` table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE release_todos (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    release_id UUID REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    CHECK (
        (release_id IS NOT NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NULL) OR
        (release_id IS NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL)
    ),
    UNIQUE(release_id),
    UNIQUE(semantic_release_id)
);
```

Key points:
- **1:1 relationship** — each release or semantic release has at most one TODO (enforced by UNIQUE constraints)
- **CHECK constraint** — exactly one of `release_id` or `semantic_release_id` must be set (same pattern as `subscriptions`)
- **CASCADE deletes** — if the underlying release is deleted, the TODO is cleaned up
- **Status transitions:** `pending` → `acknowledged` → `resolved` (one-directional, though we'll allow re-setting to any state via API)

**When are TODOs created?** During notification routing — when the `NotifyWorker` processes a release, it also inserts a `release_todos` row. For semantic releases, the same happens in the semantic release notification path. This keeps it transactional with the existing outbox pattern.

Does this schema look right?

## Design Section 2: API Endpoints

New REST endpoints under `/api/v1/todos`:

```
GET    /api/v1/todos                    — List todos (filterable by status, paginated)
GET    /api/v1/todos/{id}               — Get a single todo
PATCH  /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge   — Mark as acknowledged
PATCH  /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve       — Mark as resolved
```

Query parameters for `GET /api/v1/todos`:
- `?status=pending` / `acknowledged` / `resolved` — filter by status
- `?page=1&per_page=25` — standard pagination

The list endpoint returns todos enriched with release/semantic release metadata (project name, version, provider, repository) via JOINs — so the frontend doesn't need extra round-trips.

**Response shape** (list):
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "status": "pending",
      "release_id": "uuid",        // or null
      "semantic_release_id": "uuid", // or null
      "project_name": "Go Runtime",
      "version": "v1.22.0",
      "provider": "github",
      "repository": "golang/go",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/...",
      "release_url": "/releases/uuid",
      "urgency": "HIGH",           // from semantic report, if applicable
      "created_at": "...",
      "acknowledged_at": null,
      "resolved_at": null
    }
  ],
  "meta": { "page": 1, "per_page": 25, "total": 42 }
}
```

The acknowledge/resolve endpoints are simple PATCH operations — no request body needed. They set the timestamp and update the status.

**One-click API endpoints** (for notification button links):
```
GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge?redirect=true
GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve?redirect=true
```

These are GET endpoints (so they work as clickable links from Slack/Discord/email). The `redirect=true` param causes a 302 redirect back to the Todo page in the web portal after updating. This avoids needing POST forms in notification cards.

Does this API design look right?

## Design Section 3: Notification Card Changes

**Slack** — Add two button elements to the existing Block Kit message using `"type": "actions"` block with `"type": "button"` elements. Each button uses a `"url"` field pointing to the one-click GET endpoint. These render as native-looking Slack buttons but open a browser tab. No Slack App required since we're using URL buttons, not interactive callbacks.

```json
{
  "type": "actions",
  "elements": [
    {"type": "button", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "✅ Acknowledge"}, "url": "https://.../api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge?redirect=true"},
    {"type": "button", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "☑️ Resolve"}, "url": "https://.../api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve?redirect=true"}
  ]
}
```

**Discord** — Discord webhook embeds don't support interactive buttons without a Bot application. Instead, we'll add the links as markdown in the embed description or as a footer field: `[✅ Acknowledge](url) · [☑️ Resolve](url)`. These render as clickable links in-line.

**Email** — Add two styled HTML buttons (anchor tags styled as buttons) in the email template linking to the one-click endpoints.

**Webhook** — Add `acknowledge_url` and `resolve_url` fields to the JSON payload. Consumers can use these URLs however they want.

**Passing the TODO ID to senders:** The `NotifyWorker` creates the `release_todos` row first, then passes the TODO ID as part of the `Notification` struct (new field: `TodoID string`). Each sender uses it to construct the URLs using the existing `PUBLIC_URL` base.

Does this look right?

## Design Section 4: Frontend — Todo Tab

**New sidebar entry:** "Todo" added to the sidebar navigation between "Dashboard" and "Projects" (high visibility position), using a `CheckSquare` or `ListTodo` icon from lucide-react.

**Page: `/todo`** — Three sub-tabs for filtering:

| Tab | Filter | Purpose |
|-----|--------|---------|
| **Pending** | `status=pending` | Releases needing attention (default) |
| **Acknowledged** | `status=acknowledged` | Being handled, not yet resolved |
| **Resolved** | `status=resolved` | Completed items |

**Table columns:**
- **Project** — project name
- **Version** — version string (clickable link to release detail)
- **Type** — badge: "Release" or "Semantic" 
- **Provider** — provider badge (GitHub, Docker Hub, etc.)
- **Urgency** — urgency badge (only for semantic releases, blank for source releases)
- **Created** — when the TODO was created (relative time)
- **Actions** — "Acknowledge" / "Resolve" buttons depending on current status

**Behavior:**
- Default view shows "Pending" tab
- Clicking "Acknowledge" on a pending item transitions it to acknowledged (optimistic UI update via SWR mutation)
- Clicking "Resolve" on an acknowledged item transitions it to resolved
- Pending tab shows an "Acknowledge" button; Acknowledged tab shows a "Resolve" button; Resolved tab shows no action buttons
- Count badges on the tab headers showing how many items in each state (e.g., "Pending (3)")
- Standard pagination (Previous/Next) matching existing pages
- SSE integration — new TODO events trigger SWR revalidation so the list stays live

**Styling:** Follows existing patterns — inline styles with CSS variables, DM Sans font, `#e8601a` accent color for primary actions, familiar table layout matching the Releases page.

Does this look right?

## Design Section 5: TODO Creation Flow

**When does a TODO get created?**

A TODO is created at the same point notifications are sent — in the `NotifyWorker`. This ensures:
1. Every notified release gets a TODO (they're paired)
2. It's transactional — if the worker fails, no orphaned TODOs
3. Filtered/excluded releases don't get TODOs (they never reach the worker)

**Source releases:** In `NotifyWorker.Work()`, after fetching the release and before iterating subscriptions, insert a `release_todos` row with `release_id` set. Use `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` so re-processing a job doesn't create duplicates (the UNIQUE constraint on `release_id` protects us).

**Semantic releases:** The semantic release notification happens in the agent orchestrator after completing a semantic release. At that point, insert a `release_todos` row with `semantic_release_id` set, same `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` pattern.

**Flow diagram:**
```
New release detected
  → INSERT release (ingestion)
  → Enqueue NotifyJobArgs (transactional outbox)
  → NotifyWorker picks up job
    → INSERT release_todos (release_id, status='pending') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
    → Get todo ID (for button URLs)
    → Send notifications with acknowledge/resolve URLs
    → Check agent rules → maybe enqueue AgentJobArgs

Agent completes semantic release
  → INSERT release_todos (semantic_release_id, status='pending') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
  → Get todo ID
  → Send semantic release notifications with URLs
```

**Key detail:** The TODO must be inserted _before_ sending notifications, so the todo ID is available for constructing button URLs.

Does this flow look right?
That covers the full design. Let me write the design doc and commit it.

input.content:
# Design: Release TODO Tracking

## Overview

Add TODO tracking to releases so users can acknowledge and resolve release notifications. Notification cards include action buttons, and a new web portal tab provides a centralized view of pending/acknowledged/resolved items.

## Scope

- Applies to **both** source releases and semantic releases
- **Anonymous** actions (no user tracking)
- Notification buttons: URL-based buttons in Slack/Discord, styled links in email, URLs in webhook payload
- New "Todo" sidebar tab in the web portal

## Database Schema

New `release_todos` table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE release_todos (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    release_id UUID REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    CHECK (
        (release_id IS NOT NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NULL) OR
        (release_id IS NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL)
    ),
    UNIQUE(release_id),
    UNIQUE(semantic_release_id)
);
```

- 1:1 with release or semantic release (UNIQUE constraints)
- CHECK constraint ensures exactly one FK is set (same pattern as `subscriptions`)
- CASCADE deletes clean up when underlying release is removed
- Status: `pending` -> `acknowledged` -> `resolved`

## API Endpoints

### CRUD

```
GET    /api/v1/todos                    -- List (filterable: ?status=pending|acknowledged|resolved, paginated)
GET    /api/v1/todos/{id}              -- Get single todo
PATCH  /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge  -- Mark acknowledged
PATCH  /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve      -- Mark resolved
```

### One-Click (for notification buttons)

```
GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge?redirect=true  -- Acknowledge + 302 redirect to web portal
GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve?redirect=true       -- Resolve + 302 redirect to web portal
```

GET endpoints so they work as clickable links from Slack/Discord/email. The `redirect=true` param triggers a 302 redirect to the Todo page after updating.

### List Response Shape

```json
{
  "data": [{
    "id": "uuid",
    "status": "pending",
    "release_id": "uuid",
    "semantic_release_id": null,
    "project_name": "Go Runtime",
    "version": "v1.22.0",
    "provider": "github",
    "repository": "golang/go",
    "source_url": "https://github.com/golang/go/releases/tag/v1.22.0",
    "release_url": "/releases/uuid",
    "urgency": null,
    "created_at": "...",
    "acknowledged_at": null,
    "resolved_at": null
  }],
  "meta": { "page": 1, "per_page": 25, "total": 42 }
}
```

List endpoint returns enriched data via JOINs (project name, version, provider, etc.) to avoid extra round-trips.

## Notification Card Changes

### Slack

Add `"type": "actions"` block with URL buttons:

```json
{
  "type": "actions",
  "elements": [
    {"type": "button", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "Acknowledge"}, "url": "{PUBLIC_URL}/api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge?redirect=true"},
    {"type": "button", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "Resolve"}, "url": "{PUBLIC_URL}/api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve?redirect=true"}
  ]
}
```

URL buttons look native in Slack but open a browser. No Slack App required.

### Discord

Add markdown links in embed description (Discord webhooks don't support buttons without a Bot):

```
[Acknowledge](url) | [Resolve](url)
```

### Email

Add styled HTML anchor tags (button-styled) in the email template.

### Webhook

Add `acknowledge_url` and `resolve_url` fields to JSON payload.

### Passing TODO ID

`Notification` struct gets a new `TodoID string` field. `NotifyWorker` creates the TODO row first, then passes the ID to senders for URL construction.

## TODO Creation Flow

TODOs are created during notification routing:

**Source releases:** In `NotifyWorker.Work()`, before sending notifications:
1. `INSERT INTO release_todos (release_id) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`
2. Get the todo ID
3. Pass to senders via `Notification.TodoID`

**Semantic releases:** In the agent orchestrator's notification path, after completing a semantic release:
1. `INSERT INTO release_todos (semantic_release_id) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`
2. Get the todo ID
3. Pass to senders via `Notification.TodoID`

`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` ensures idempotency (safe for job retries).

## Frontend: Todo Tab

### Sidebar

New "Todo" entry between Dashboard and Projects, using `ListTodo` icon from lucide-react.

### Page: `/todo`

Three sub-tabs with count badges:

| Tab | Filter | Default |
|-----|--------|---------|
| Pending (N) | `status=pending` | Yes |
| Acknowledged (N) | `status=acknowledged` | |
| Resolved (N) | `status=resolved` | |

### Table Columns

| Column | Content |
|--------|---------|
| Project | Project name |
| Version | Version string (links to release detail) |
| Type | Badge: "Release" or "Semantic" |
| Provider | Provider badge |
| Urgency | Urgency badge (semantic only) |
| Created | Relative timestamp |
| Actions | Acknowledge/Resolve buttons |

### Behavior

- Pending tab: "Acknowledge" button per row
- Acknowledged tab: "Resolve" button per row
- Resolved tab: no action buttons
- Optimistic UI updates via SWR mutation
- SSE revalidation for live updates
- Standard pagination (Previous/Next)
- Follows existing styling patterns (inline styles, CSS variables, DM Sans)

`Notification` struct gets a new `TodoID string` field. `NotifyWorker` creates the TODO row first, then passes the ID to senders for URL construction.

## TODO Creation Flow

TODOs are created during notification routing:

**Source releases:** In `NotifyWorker.Work()`, before sending notifications:
1. `INSERT INTO release_todos (release_id) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`
2. Get the todo ID
3. Pass to senders via `Notification.TodoID`

**Semantic releases:** In the agent orchestrator's notification path, after completing a semantic release:
1. `INSERT INTO release_todos (semantic_release_id) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`
2. Get the todo ID
3. Pass to senders via `Notification.TodoID`

`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` ensures idempotency (safe for job retries).

## Frontend: Todo Tab

### Sidebar

New "Todo" entry between Dashboard and Projects, using `ListTodo` icon from lucide-react.

### Page: `/todo`

Three sub-tabs with count badges:

| Tab | Filter | Default |
|-----|--------|---------|
| Pending (N) | `status=pending` | Yes |
| Acknowledged (N) | `status=acknowledged` | |
| Resolved (N) | `status=resolved` | |

### Table Columns

| Column | Content |
|--------|---------|
| Project | Project name |
| Version | Version string (links to release detail) |
| Type | Badge: "Release" or "Semantic" |
| Provider | Provider badge |
| Urgency | Urgency badge (semantic only) |
| Created | Relative timestamp |
| Actions | Acknowledge/Resolve buttons |

### Behavior

- Pending tab: "Acknowledge" button per row
- Acknowledged tab: "Resolve" button per row
- Resolved tab: no action buttons
- Optimistic UI updates via SWR mutation
- SSE revalidation for live updates
- Standard pagination (Previous/Next)
- Follows existing styling patterns (inline styles, CSS variables, DM Sans)
